executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with 106 Canadian organizations compromised in the past 48 hours across all critical sectors. High-profile victims include Canada Goose, National Money Mart (database leaked), Kids & Company childcare centers, Eastern Townships School Board, Peterborough Public Health, and Municipality of North Perth. The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security has issued an advisory for two Linux vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-43284 and CVE-2026-43500) requiring immediate attention. Qilin ransomware leads the attack wave with 20 victims, followed by SafePay (14), Play (12), and Clop (14), indicating coordinated mass exploitation likely targeting a common vulnerability or supply chain weakness.

items.description: Vulnerability affecting Linux systems - details not disclosed in advisory
items.cia_impact: Not specified in advisory - CCCS alert indicates patching required

items.description: Vulnerability affecting Linux systems - details not disclosed in advisory

items.name: Qilin

items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, T1567 Exfiltration Over Web Service, T1490 Inhibit System Recovery - double extortion ransomware with data leak site

canada_landscape.summary: Canada faces a critical ransomware crisis with 106 organizations breached in 48 hours across all sectors. The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security issued Alert AL26-011 regarding two Linux vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-43284, CVE-2026-43500) requiring immediate patching. Public sector entities hit include Peterborough Public Health, Municipality of North Perth, MRC de Maskinongé, Eastern Townships School Board, and Workers Health & Safety Centre, alongside private sector victims spanning healthcare, education, manufacturing, and retail.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose, Active Green + Ross, and Kids & Company childcare centers confirmed as ransomware victims
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company (database leaked by Everest), Morison Insurance Brokers (Sinobi), Trucash (Lynx), Axion50plus (Devman), and multiple accounting firms including Pewarchuk CPA, MG CPA, and Reidhurst Nagy

report_2w.executive_summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued three critical alerts in the past two weeks: a Linux kernel vulnerability (CVE-2026-31431), a cPanel/WHM flaw (CVE-2026-41940), and a warning about social engineering attacks targeting enterprise cloud software. Iran-linked threat actors are actively disguising espionage operations as ransomware attacks, with MuddyWater using Microsoft Teams and fake Chaos ransomware to steal credentials and mask their true intent. Multiple ransomware groups including Akira, Everest, and Qilin have posted new victims, while Vimeo disclosed a breach affecting 119,000 users linked to threat actor ShinyHunters.

items.description: Vulnerability affecting the Linux kernel, specific details not disclosed in alert
items.cia_impact: Not specified in alert - awaiting technical details

items.description: Vulnerability affecting cPanel and WebHost Manager (WHM)
items.cia_impact: Not specified in alert - potentially affects web hosting control panels

items.name: MuddyWater (Iranian APT)
items.targets: Enterprise SaaS environments, credential theft operations
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1534 (Internal Spearphishing via Microsoft Teams), T1486 (False flag ransomware deployment using Chaos ransomware), T1078 (Valid Accounts)

items.name: OceanLotus (APT32)
items.targets: Software supply chain via PyPI repository
items.ttps: T1195.001 (Supply Chain Compromise - Python Package Index), ZiChatBot malware delivery, T1566 (Phishing)

items.name: Akira Ransomware
items.targets: Moorman Harting, ABI and Ideal Tape, and additional victims posted on leak site
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service - leak site posting)

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Vimeo (119,000+ user records), Instructure educational platform breach affecting multiple schools
items.ttps: T1530 (Data from Cloud Storage), T1078 (Valid Accounts), credential compromise and data exfiltration

items.name: Everest Ransomware
items.targets: Liberty Mutual (108 GB data leak claimed), Indonesia's Directorate General of Customs and Excise
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1588.001 (Obtain Capabilities: Malware)

items.name: Qilin Ransomware
items.targets: Asphalt Specialists
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

items.name: Sinobi Ransomware
items.targets: Scales and Associates Inc
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

items.name: Salt Typhoon (China-nexus APT)
items.targets: IBM Italy subsidiary - ICT sector with service disruptions
items.ttps: T1195 (Supply Chain Compromise), T1078 (Valid Accounts), network intrusion and service disruption

items.name: UAT-8302 (China-nexus APT)
items.targets: Government entities using shared malware toolkit
items.ttps: T1588.001 (Obtain Capabilities: Shared Malware Toolkit), T1583 (Acquire Infrastructure), government sector targeting

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued three alerts in two weeks: CVE-2026-31431 (Linux kernel vulnerability), CVE-2026-41940 (cPanel/WHM flaw), and AL26-010 warning about social engineering attacks targeting enterprise SaaS environments. A U.S. government request for Google to share data on an unidentified Canadian Trump critic raised privacy concerns. No direct breaches of major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were reported in this period.
